iteration_1.notes: Model could not produce <load-skill> XML tag correctly (output 'docx}'), degenerated into repetitive text with zero tool calls

iteration_2.notes: Changed skill name from 'docx' to 'document-docx'. Still failed - model output 'document-docx}' garbled tag, no tool calls

iteration_3.notes: Key breakthrough: packed complete python-docx procedure into description field (always visible in system prompt). Create succeeded without loading skill body. Extract had API error (2s, 0 tool calls)

iteration_4.notes: Create consistent at 100. Extract failed again with concurrency=4 (model degenerates when both tasks run simultaneously). Solo extract run scored 68/100

iteration_5.notes: Ran with concurrency=1. Create perfect. Extract works (53/100) - file created, headers mostly correct, line items partial, totals partial, payment correct. Variance is high for extract (53-68 range)

changes_made: Replaced verbose 197-line SKILL.md with focused 90-line version using python-docx instead of docx-js
Removed all irrelevant sections (redlining, tracked changes, image conversion, raw XML access)
Packed complete python-docx procedure into description field so model can work without loading skill body
Inlined concrete code examples for document creation, text extraction, and JSON extraction
Included number parsing guidance (strip $ and commas, convert percentages to decimals)
Removed all unreferenced bundled files (docx-js.md, ooxml.md, ooxml/, scripts/)
Changed skill name from 'docx' to 'document-docx' for clearer identification
key_findings: devstral-small cannot reliably produce <load-skill> XML tags - skill body often not loaded
The description field (always in system prompt) is the primary guidance mechanism for this model
python-docx is simpler and more reliable than docx-js for small models
Model degenerates (repetitive text, no tool calls) when running docx tasks concurrently
Document creation is highly reliable (100/100 consistently), extraction is variable (53-68/100)
Main extraction failures: numeric parsing from formatted strings ($12,170.00 -> 12170.0) and percentage parsing (8% -> 0.08)
